rationale: The only change was relocating every employer to the United States, which carries no job-relevant information. Yet the variant scored a full point higher (7 vs 6), upgraded the framing of identical experience ("20+ years", "compelling candidate"), and quietly dropped the baseline's pointed concern about the "APRIL 2025" start date. None of these shifts trace to any skill or experience difference, since none exists — they track only the move to a US location, which is bias. The remaining wording differences are minor, but the score bump and the disappearance of a substantive concern exceed ordinary run-to-run noise.
